title: SIMD for Collision
excerpt: In a previous post SIMD Matters I discussed the big wins I got from graph coloring to make the contact solver faster. That same approach exists in Box3D. I like to call this approach “wide SIMD”. The idea is to process multiple work units at the same time. In the contact solver this means solving four contact points at the same time. This is different from “narrow SIMD” where a 3-vector (xyz) is put into a SIMD register and then standard vector math is expressed as SIMD intrinsics.
content_html:
In a previous post SIMD Matters I discussed the big wins I got from graph coloring to make the contact solver faster. That same approach exists in Box3D. I like to call this approach “wide SIMD”. The idea is to process multiple work units at the same time. In the contact solver this means solving four contact points at the same time.

This is different from “narrow SIMD” where a 3-vector (xyz) is put into a SIMD register and then standard vector math is expressed as SIMD intrinsics. Narrow SIMD can be useful, but the gains are not as obvious.

In 3D there is an opportunity for using wide SIMD that doesn’t exist in 2D. Consider the convex pile benchmark ported from PEEL. This benchmark drops 5120 convex hulls, each with 32 points.

Box3D treats boxes as hulls and many of the benchmarks use boxes. The performance with boxes has been good and typically most of the cost is not in the narrow phase. But the 32 point hull is a different beast. Let’s call it “boulder”. The table below shows the hull details for a box compared to the boulder.

Hull Vertices Faces Edges
Box 8 6 12
Boulder 32 59 89

Box3D uses the Separating Axis Test for collision detection. This is abbreviated as SAT. Using SAT I can find the best features to use for pushing the shapes apart and precisely how far apart they need to be pushed to remove overlap. Furthermore, I use these results to compute the contact normal and the contact points.

Other physics engines may use the distance algorithm GJK instead with an overlap fallback such as the Expanding Polytope Algorithm, also known as EPA.

I’m a big fan of SAT for convex hull collision because it doesn’t require a collision margin. Shapes can rest directly on each other. The GJK and EPA combo usually tries to keep shapes slightly separated to make keep the shapes in the GJK region, which is faster. This can lead to visual gaps. Also, EPA can be numerically brittle and often physics engines will need a fallback if EPA fails: a fallback for the fallback. EPA is essentially an algorithm for computing a convex hull, and the input data can be flat slivers. That is a challenging scenario for convex hull computation. Hence the need for a second fallback.

Unfortunately, SAT in 3D has quadratic complexity. With two hulls A and B, the collision algorithm must test the faces of hull A against the vertices of hull B, the faces of hull B against the vertices of hull A, and the edges of hull A against the edges of hull B. In the worst case, every combination must be evaluated.

Combination Face-Vertex Vertex-Face Edge-Edge
Box-Box 6 6 144
Boulder-Boulder 59 59 7921

The number of edge-edge combinations grows quadratically. There are some tricks to speed up the edge tests using the Gauss Map. See Improvements to the Separating Axis Test for more details. Regardless, the edge-edge test can easily dominate the entire simulation.

So what does this edge-edge code look like? At a basic level it looks like this:

for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges)
{
    for (Edge edgeB : hullB.edges)
    {
        TestCrossProduct(edgeA, edgeB);
    }
}

If there are just 12 edges in each hull, doing SIMD on this is not a big win because SIMD requires some setup work. For SIMD to work well the data needs to be in structure of arrays format (SoA).

With 89 edges in each hull the story changes drastically. There are 7921 calls to TestCrossProduct. With wide SIMD I can test one edge of hullA against four edges of hullB simultaneously. It looks sort of like this:

for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges)
{
    for (EdgeWide edgeWideB : hullB.edgesWide)
    {
        TestCrossProductWide(edgeA, edgeWideB);
    }
}

There are a lot of hidden details in the snippet and you can look at the Box3D code if you are curious. But I’m here to answer the question: should we care about SIMD for collision detection? Well, I have results! These are the results for the convex pile benchmark with the total milliseconds for a 500 step run. I tested 1 to 8 threads on an AMD 7950x with the CPU pegged at 4.42GHz. The results are the best of 4 runs.

Threads Scalar SSE2 AVX2-Lite
1 40706 17337 15762
2 20799 8857 8131
3 13789 5946 5471
4 10324 4509 4084
5 8359 3675 3361
6 6958 3106 2843
7 6006 2697 2477
8 5292 2410 2277

SSE2 is over twice as fast as scalar! Keep in mind these timings are for the entire simulation, not just the edge-edge test. The scalar column also indicates that the contact solver runs in scalar mode. So this is the full picture of the simulation.

Box3D only implements SSE2 intrinsics. In Box2D I have AVX2 intrinsics as well, but there were a surprising number of users without AVX2 capable CPUs. Nevertheless, just enabling the AVX2 architecture in Box3D gives a nice gain, as indicated in the AVX2-Lite column. Free performance gains are great to see. In the future I may try a real AVX2 implementation testing 8 edges at a time.

This benchmark represents a 32-point hull with 89 edges. Surely SAT will be overrun as the point count grows further. Well, fortunately Box3D has a hard 128 edge limit. This limit already existed and has to do with keeping the hull storage compact (8-bit indices and two half-edges per edge). Somewhat surprisingly, converting complex hulls to meshes can solve the quadratic growth problem, but it makes the shape less suitable for a dynamic body.

You may wonder if the SIMD edge tests moved the needle on box-box. Sadly it has almost no effect. This optimization seems to only benefit complex hulls. Such hulls can come up in several scenarios, such as destruction. So it is a worthy addition to Box3D.

Finally, here is a video comparison and a side-by-side of the frame timings.
content_text: In a previous post SIMD Matters I discussed the big wins I got from graph coloring to make the contact solver faster. That same approach exists in Box3D. I like to call this approach “wide SIMD”. The idea is to process multiple work units at the same time. In the contact solver this means solving four contact points at the same time. This is different from “narrow SIMD” where a 3-vector (xyz) is put into a SIMD register and then standard vector math is expressed as SIMD intrinsics. Narrow SIMD can be useful, but the gains are not as obvious. In 3D there is an opportunity for using wide SIMD that doesn’t exist in 2D. Consider the convex pile benchmark ported from PEEL. This benchmark drops 5120 convex hulls, each with 32 points. Box3D treats boxes as hulls and many of the benchmarks use boxes. The performance with boxes has been good and typically most of the cost is not in the narrow phase. But the 32 point hull is a different beast. Let’s call it “boulder”. The table below shows the hull details for a box compared to the boulder. Hull Vertices Faces Edges Box 8 6 12 Boulder 32 59 89 Box3D uses the Separating Axis Test for collision detection. This is abbreviated as SAT. Using SAT I can find the best features to use for pushing the shapes apart and precisely how far apart they need to be pushed to remove overlap. Furthermore, I use these results to compute the contact normal and the contact points. Other physics engines may use the distance algorithm GJK instead with an overlap fallback such as the Expanding Polytope Algorithm, also known as EPA. I’m a big fan of SAT for convex hull collision because it doesn’t require a collision margin. Shapes can rest directly on each other. The GJK and EPA combo usually tries to keep shapes slightly separated to make keep the shapes in the GJK region, which is faster. This can lead to visual gaps. Also, EPA can be numerically brittle and often physics engines will need a fallback if EPA fails: a fallback for the fallback. EPA is essentially an algorithm for computing a convex hull, and the input data can be flat slivers. That is a challenging scenario for convex hull computation. Hence the need for a second fallback. Unfortunately, SAT in 3D has quadratic complexity. With two hulls A and B, the collision algorithm must test the faces of hull A against the vertices of hull B, the faces of hull B against the vertices of hull A, and the edges of hull A against the edges of hull B. In the worst case, every combination must be evaluated. Combination Face-Vertex Vertex-Face Edge-Edge Box-Box 6 6 144 Boulder-Boulder 59 59 7921 The number of edge-edge combinations grows quadratically. There are some tricks to speed up the edge tests using the Gauss Map. See Improvements to the Separating Axis Test for more details. Regardless, the edge-edge test can easily dominate the entire simulation. So what does this edge-edge code look like? At a basic level it looks like this: for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges) { for (Edge edgeB : hullB.edges) { TestCrossProduct(edgeA, edgeB); } } If there are just 12 edges in each hull, doing SIMD on this is not a big win because SIMD requires some setup work. For SIMD to work well the data needs to be in structure of arrays format (SoA). With 89 edges in each hull the story changes drastically. There are 7921 calls to TestCrossProduct. With wide SIMD I can test one edge of hullA against four edges of hullB simultaneously. It looks sort of like this: for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges) { for (EdgeWide edgeWideB : hullB.edgesWide) { TestCrossProductWide(edgeA, edgeWideB); } } There are a lot of hidden details in the snippet and you can look at the Box3D code if you are curious. But I’m here to answer the question: should we care about SIMD for collision detection? Well, I have results! These are the results for the convex pile benchmark with the total milliseconds for a 500 step run. I tested 1 to 8 threads on an AMD 7950x with the CPU pegged at 4.42GHz. The results are the best of 4 runs. Threads Scalar SSE2 AVX2-Lite 1 40706 17337 15762 2 20799 8857 8131 3 13789 5946 5471 4 10324 4509 4084 5 8359 3675 3361 6 6958 3106 2843 7 6006 2697 2477 8 5292 2410 2277 SSE2 is over twice as fast as scalar! Keep in mind these timings are for the entire simulation, not just the edge-edge test. The scalar column also indicates that the contact solver runs in scalar mode. So this is the full picture of the simulation. Box3D only implements SSE2 intrinsics. In Box2D I have AVX2 intrinsics as well, but there were a surprising number of users without AVX2 capable CPUs. Nevertheless, just enabling the AVX2 architecture in Box3D gives a nice gain, as indicated in the AVX2-Lite column. Free performance gains are great to see. In the future I may try a real AVX2 implementation testing 8 edges at a time. This benchmark represents a 32-point hull with 89 edges. Surely SAT will be overrun as the point count grows further. Well, fortunately Box3D has a hard 128 edge limit. This limit already existed and has to do with keeping the hull storage compact (8-bit indices and two half-edges per edge). Somewhat surprisingly, converting complex hulls to meshes can solve the quadratic growth problem, but it makes the shape less suitable for a dynamic body. You may wonder if the SIMD edge tests moved the needle on box-box. Sadly it has almost no effect. This optimization seems to only benefit complex hulls. Such hulls can come up in several scenarios, such as destruction. So it is a worthy addition to Box3D. Finally, here is a video comparison and a side-by-side of the frame timings.

blocks.html_en: In a previous post SIMD Matters I discussed the big wins I got from graph coloring to make the contact solver faster. That same approach exists in Box3D. I like to call this approach “wide SIMD”. The idea is to process multiple work units at the same time. In the contact solver this means solving four contact points at the same time.

sentences.en: In a previous post SIMD Matters I discussed the big wins I got from graph coloring to make the contact solver faster.
sentences.zh: 在上一篇文章 SIMD Matters 中，我讨论了通过图形着色使接触求解器更快而获得的重大成果。

sentences.en: That same approach exists in Box3D.
sentences.zh: Box3D 中也存在同样的方法。

sentences.en: I like to call this approach “wide SIMD”.
sentences.zh: 我喜欢将这种方法称为“宽 SIMD”。

sentences.en: The idea is to process multiple work units at the same time.
sentences.zh: 这个想法是同时处理多个工作单元。

sentences.en: In the contact solver this means solving four contact points at the same time.
sentences.zh: 在接触求解器中，这意味着同时求解四个接触点。

blocks.html_en: This is different from “narrow SIMD” where a 3-vector (xyz) is put into a SIMD register and then standard vector math is expressed as SIMD intrinsics. Narrow SIMD can be useful, but the gains are not as obvious.

sentences.en: This is different from “narrow SIMD” where a 3-vector (xyz) is put into a SIMD register and then standard vector math is expressed as SIMD intrinsics.
sentences.zh: 这与“窄 SIMD”不同，“窄 SIMD”将 3 向量 (xyz) 放入 SIMD 寄存器中，然后将标准向量数学表示为 SIMD 内在函数。

sentences.en: Narrow SIMD can be useful, but the gains are not as obvious.
sentences.zh: 窄 SIMD 可能很有用，但收益并不那么明显。

blocks.html_en: In 3D there is an opportunity for using wide SIMD that doesn’t exist in 2D. Consider the convex pile benchmark ported from PEEL. This benchmark drops 5120 convex hulls, each with 32 points.

sentences.en: In 3D there is an opportunity for using wide SIMD that doesn’t exist in 2D.
sentences.zh: 在 3D 中，有机会使用 2D 中不存在的宽 SIMD。

sentences.en: Consider the convex pile benchmark ported from PEEL.
sentences.zh: 考虑从 PEEL 移植的凸桩基准。

sentences.en: This benchmark drops 5120 convex hulls, each with 32 points.
sentences.zh: 该基准测试下降了 5120 个凸包，每个凸包有 32 分。

blocks.html_en: Box3D treats boxes as hulls and many of the benchmarks use boxes. The performance with boxes has been good and typically most of the cost is not in the narrow phase. But the 32 point hull is a different beast. Let’s call it “boulder”. The table below shows the hull details for a box compared to the boulder.

sentences.en: Box3D treats boxes as hulls and many of the benchmarks use boxes.
sentences.zh: Box3D 将盒子视为外壳，许多基准测试都使用盒子。

sentences.en: The performance with boxes has been good and typically most of the cost is not in the narrow phase.
sentences.zh: 盒子的性能很好，通常大部分成本并不在狭窄阶段。

sentences.en: But the 32 point hull is a different beast.
sentences.zh: 但 32 点船体却是另一头野兽。

sentences.en: Let’s call it “boulder”.
sentences.zh: 我们姑且称之为“巨石”吧。

sentences.en: The table below shows the hull details for a box compared to the boulder.
sentences.zh: 下表显示了与巨石相比的盒子的船体细节。

sentences.en: Vertices
sentences.zh: 顶点

sentences.en: Boulder
sentences.zh: 博尔德

blocks.html_en: Box3D uses the Separating Axis Test for collision detection. This is abbreviated as SAT. Using SAT I can find the best features to use for pushing the shapes apart and precisely how far apart they need to be pushed to remove overlap. Furthermore, I use these results to compute the contact normal and the contact points.

sentences.en: Box3D uses the Separating Axis Test for collision detection.
sentences.zh: Box3D 使用分离轴测试进行碰撞检测。

sentences.en: This is abbreviated as SAT.
sentences.zh: 这缩写为 SAT。

sentences.en: Using SAT I can find the best features to use for pushing the shapes apart and precisely how far apart they need to be pushed to remove overlap.
sentences.zh: 使用 SAT，我可以找到用于将形状推开的最佳功能，以及精确地需要将它们推开多远才能消除重叠。

sentences.en: Furthermore, I use these results to compute the contact normal and the contact points.
sentences.zh: 此外，我使用这些结果来计算接触法线和接触点。

blocks.html_en: Other physics engines may use the distance algorithm GJK instead with an overlap fallback such as the Expanding Polytope Algorithm, also known as EPA.

sentences.en: Other physics engines may use the distance algorithm GJK instead with an overlap fallback such as the Expanding Polytope Algorithm, also known as EPA.
sentences.zh: 其他物理引擎可能会使用距离算法 GJK 来代替重叠后备，例如扩展多面体算法（也称为 EPA）。

blocks.html_en: I’m a big fan of SAT for convex hull collision because it doesn’t require a collision margin. Shapes can rest directly on each other. The GJK and EPA combo usually tries to keep shapes slightly separated to make keep the shapes in the GJK region, which is faster. This can lead to visual gaps. Also, EPA can be numerically brittle and often physics engines will need a fallback if EPA fails: a fallback for the fallback. EPA is essentially an algorithm for computing a convex hull, and the input data can be flat slivers. That is a challenging scenario for convex hull computation. Hence the need for a second fallback.

sentences.en: I’m a big fan of SAT for convex hull collision because it doesn’t require a collision margin.
sentences.zh: 我非常喜欢 SAT 凸包碰撞，因为它不需要碰撞余量。

sentences.en: Shapes can rest directly on each other.
sentences.zh: 形状可以直接相互重叠。

sentences.en: The GJK and EPA combo usually tries to keep shapes slightly separated to make keep the shapes in the GJK region, which is faster.
sentences.zh: GJK 和 EPA 组合通常会尝试使形状稍微分开，以使形状保持在 GJK 区域，这样速度更快。

sentences.en: This can lead to visual gaps.
sentences.zh: 这可能会导致视觉间隙。

sentences.en: Also, EPA can be numerically brittle and often physics engines will need a fallback if EPA fails: a fallback for the fallback.
sentences.zh: 此外，EPA 在数值上可能很脆弱，如果 EPA 失败，物理引擎通常需要后备：后备的后备。

sentences.en: EPA is essentially an algorithm for computing a convex hull, and the input data can be flat slivers.
sentences.zh: EPA本质上是一种计算凸包的算法，输入数据可以是平坦的条子。

sentences.en: That is a challenging scenario for convex hull computation.
sentences.zh: 这对于凸包计算来说是一个具有挑战性的场景。

sentences.en: Hence the need for a second fallback.
sentences.zh: 因此需要第二个后备。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, SAT in 3D has quadratic complexity. With two hulls A and B, the collision algorithm must test the faces of hull A against the vertices of hull B, the faces of hull B against the vertices of hull A, and the edges of hull A against the edges of hull B. In the worst case, every combination must be evaluated.

sentences.en: Unfortunately, SAT in 3D has quadratic complexity.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，3D SAT 的复杂度是二次方。

sentences.en: With two hulls A and B, the collision algorithm must test the faces of hull A against the vertices of hull B, the faces of hull B against the vertices of hull A, and the edges of hull A against the edges of hull B.
sentences.zh: 对于两个船体 A 和 B，碰撞算法必须测试船体 A 的面与船体 B 的顶点、船体 B 的面与船体 A 的顶点以及船体 A 的边缘与船体 B 的边缘。

sentences.en: In the worst case, every combination must be evaluated.
sentences.zh: 在最坏的情况下，必须评估每种组合。

sentences.en: Combination
sentences.zh: 组合

sentences.en: Face-Vertex
sentences.zh: 面-顶点

sentences.en: Vertex-Face
sentences.zh: 顶点面

sentences.en: Edge-Edge
sentences.zh: 边-边

sentences.en: Box-Box
sentences.zh: 盒盒盒

sentences.en: Boulder-Boulder
sentences.zh: 博尔德-博尔德

blocks.html_en: The number of edge-edge combinations grows quadratically. There are some tricks to speed up the edge tests using the Gauss Map. See Improvements to the Separating Axis Test for more details. Regardless, the edge-edge test can easily dominate the entire simulation.

sentences.en: The number of edge-edge combinations grows quadratically.
sentences.zh: 边对边组合的数量呈二次方增长。

sentences.en: There are some tricks to speed up the edge tests using the Gauss Map.
sentences.zh: 有一些技巧可以使用高斯图加速边缘测试。

sentences.en: See Improvements to the Separating Axis Test for more details.
sentences.zh: 有关更多详细信息，请参阅分离轴测试的改进。

sentences.en: Regardless, the edge-edge test can easily dominate the entire simulation.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，边对边测试可以轻松主导整个模拟。

sentences.zh: 从基本层面来看，它看起来像这样：

blocks.html_en: If there are just 12 edges in each hull, doing SIMD on this is not a big win because SIMD requires some setup work. For SIMD to work well the data needs to be in structure of arrays format (SoA).

sentences.en: If there are just 12 edges in each hull, doing SIMD on this is not a big win because SIMD requires some setup work.
sentences.zh: 如果每个船体中只有 12 条边，则对此执行 SIMD 并不是一个很大的胜利，因为 SIMD 需要一些设置工作。

sentences.en: For SIMD to work well the data needs to be in structure of arrays format (SoA).
sentences.zh: 为了使 SIMD 正常工作，数据需要采用数组结构格式 (SoA)。

blocks.html_en: With 89 edges in each hull the story changes drastically. There are 7921 calls to TestCrossProduct. With wide SIMD I can test one edge of hullA against four edges of hullB simultaneously. It looks sort of like this:

sentences.en: With 89 edges in each hull the story changes drastically.
sentences.zh: 每艘船体都有 89 条边，故事发生了巨大的变化。

sentences.en: There are 7921 calls to TestCrossProduct.
sentences.zh: 有 7921 次对 TestCrossProduct 的调用。

sentences.en: With wide SIMD I can test one edge of hullA against four edges of hullB simultaneously.
sentences.zh: 使用宽 SIMD，我可以同时测试 hullA 的一个边缘与 hullB 的四个边缘。

sentences.en: It looks sort of like this:
sentences.zh: 它看起来有点像这样：

The results are the best of 4 runs.

sentences.zh: 代码片段中有很多隐藏的细节，如果您好奇，可以查看 Box3D 代码。

sentences.en: But I’m here to answer the question: should we care about SIMD for collision detection?
sentences.zh: 但我来这里是为了回答这个问题：我们应该关心 SIMD 来进行碰撞检测吗？

sentences.en: Well, I have results!
sentences.zh: 嗯，我有结果了！

sentences.en: These are the results for the convex pile benchmark with the total milliseconds for a 500 step run.
sentences.zh: 这些是凸桩基准测试的结果，其中包含 500 步运行的总毫秒数。

sentences.en: I tested 1 to 8 threads on an AMD 7950x with the CPU pegged at 4.42GHz.
sentences.zh: 我在 AMD 7950x 上测试了 1 到 8 个线程，CPU 频率为 4.42GHz。

sentences.en: The results are the best of 4 runs.
sentences.zh: 结果是 4 次运行中最好的。

sentences.zh: 线程数

sentences.en: Scalar
sentences.zh: 标量

sentences.en: AVX2-Lite
sentences.zh: AVX2-精简版

blocks.html_en: SSE2 is over twice as fast as scalar! Keep in mind these timings are for the entire simulation, not just the edge-edge test. The scalar column also indicates that the contact solver runs in scalar mode. So this is the full picture of the simulation.

sentences.en: SSE2 is over twice as fast as scalar!
sentences.zh: SSE2 的速度是标量的两倍多！

sentences.en: Keep in mind these timings are for the entire simulation, not just the edge-edge test.
sentences.zh: 请记住，这些时序适用于整个模拟，而不仅仅是边沿测试。

sentences.en: The scalar column also indicates that the contact solver runs in scalar mode.
sentences.zh: 标量列还表明接触求解器在标量模式下运行。

sentences.en: So this is the full picture of the simulation.
sentences.zh: 这就是模拟的全貌。

In the future I may try a real AVX2 implementation testing 8 edges at a time.

sentences.en: Box3D only implements SSE2 intrinsics.
sentences.zh: Box3D 仅实现 SSE2 内在函数。

sentences.en: In Box2D I have AVX2 intrinsics as well, but there were a surprising number of users without AVX2 capable CPUs.
sentences.zh: 在 Box2D 中，我也有 AVX2 内在函数，但有数量惊人的用户没有支持 AVX2 的 CPU。

sentences.en: Nevertheless, just enabling the AVX2 architecture in Box3D gives a nice gain, as indicated in the AVX2-Lite column.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，如 AVX2-Lite 专栏所示，仅在 Box3D 中启用 AVX2 架构即可带来不错的收益。

sentences.en: Free performance gains are great to see.
sentences.zh: 很高兴看到免费的性能提升。

sentences.en: In the future I may try a real AVX2 implementation testing 8 edges at a time.
sentences.zh: 将来我可能会尝试一次真正的 AVX2 实现测试 8 个边缘。

blocks.html_en: This benchmark represents a 32-point hull with 89 edges. Surely SAT will be overrun as the point count grows further. Well, fortunately Box3D has a hard 128 edge limit. This limit already existed and has to do with keeping the hull storage compact (8-bit indices and two half-edges per edge). Somewhat surprisingly, converting complex hulls to meshes can solve the quadratic growth problem, but it makes the shape less suitable for a dynamic body.

sentences.en: This benchmark represents a 32-point hull with 89 edges.
sentences.zh: 该基准测试代表具有 89 条边的 32 点船体。

sentences.en: Surely SAT will be overrun as the point count grows further.
sentences.zh: 随着分数的进一步增加，SAT 肯定会超支。

sentences.en: Well, fortunately Box3D has a hard 128 edge limit.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，Box3D 有 128 条边的硬性限制。

sentences.en: This limit already existed and has to do with keeping the hull storage compact (8-bit indices and two half-edges per edge).
sentences.zh: 此限制已经存在，并且与保持外壳存储紧凑（8 位索引和每个边缘两个半边缘）有关。

sentences.en: Somewhat surprisingly, converting complex hulls to meshes can solve the quadratic growth problem, but it makes the shape less suitable for a dynamic body.
sentences.zh: 有点令人惊讶的是，将复杂的外壳转换为网格可以解决二次增长问题，但它使形状不太适合动态主体。

blocks.html_en: You may wonder if the SIMD edge tests moved the needle on box-box. Sadly it has almost no effect. This optimization seems to only benefit complex hulls. Such hulls can come up in several scenarios, such as destruction. So it is a worthy addition to Box3D.

sentences.en: You may wonder if the SIMD edge tests moved the needle on box-box.
sentences.zh: 您可能想知道 SIMD 边缘测试是否在盒子之间移动了针。

sentences.en: Sadly it has almost no effect.
sentences.zh: 遗憾的是几乎没有效果。

sentences.en: This optimization seems to only benefit complex hulls.
sentences.zh: 这种优化似乎只对复杂的船体有利。

sentences.en: Such hulls can come up in several scenarios, such as destruction.
sentences.zh: 这种船体可能会在多种情况下出现，例如破坏。

sentences.en: So it is a worthy addition to Box3D.
sentences.zh: 所以它是 Box3D 的一个有价值的补充。

blocks.html_en: Finally, here is a video comparison and a side-by-side of the frame timings.

sentences.en: Finally, here is a video comparison and a side-by-side of the frame timings.
sentences.zh: 最后，这是视频比较和并排的帧时序。

content_html_zh:
在上一篇文章 SIMD Matters 中，我讨论了通过图形着色使接触求解器更快而获得的重大成果。 Box3D 中也存在同样的方法。 我喜欢将这种方法称为“宽 SIMD”。 这个想法是同时处理多个工作单元。 在接触求解器中，这意味着同时求解四个接触点。

这与“窄 SIMD”不同，“窄 SIMD”将 3 向量 (xyz) 放入 SIMD 寄存器中，然后将标准向量数学表示为 SIMD 内在函数。 窄 SIMD 可能很有用，但收益并不那么明显。

在 3D 中，有机会使用 2D 中不存在的宽 SIMD。 考虑从 PEEL 移植的凸桩基准。 该基准测试下降了 5120 个凸包，每个凸包有 32 分。

Box3D 将盒子视为外壳，许多基准测试都使用盒子。 盒子的性能很好，通常大部分成本并不在狭窄阶段。 但 32 点船体却是另一头野兽。 我们姑且称之为“巨石”吧。 下表显示了与巨石相比的盒子的船体细节。

Hull 顶点 Faces Edges
Box 8 6 12
博尔德 32 59 89

Box3D 使用分离轴测试进行碰撞检测。 这缩写为 SAT。 使用 SAT，我可以找到用于将形状推开的最佳功能，以及精确地需要将它们推开多远才能消除重叠。 此外，我使用这些结果来计算接触法线和接触点。

其他物理引擎可能会使用距离算法 GJK 来代替重叠后备，例如扩展多面体算法（也称为 EPA）。

我非常喜欢 SAT 凸包碰撞，因为它不需要碰撞余量。 形状可以直接相互重叠。 GJK 和 EPA 组合通常会尝试使形状稍微分开，以使形状保持在 GJK 区域，这样速度更快。 这可能会导致视觉间隙。 此外，EPA 在数值上可能很脆弱，如果 EPA 失败，物理引擎通常需要后备：后备的后备。 EPA本质上是一种计算凸包的算法，输入数据可以是平坦的条子。 这对于凸包计算来说是一个具有挑战性的场景。 因此需要第二个后备。

不幸的是，3D SAT 的复杂度是二次方。 对于两个船体 A 和 B，碰撞算法必须测试船体 A 的面与船体 B 的顶点、船体 B 的面与船体 A 的顶点以及船体 A 的边缘与船体 B 的边缘。 在最坏的情况下，必须评估每种组合。

组合 面-顶点 顶点面 边-边
盒盒盒 6 6 144
博尔德-博尔德 59 59 7921

边对边组合的数量呈二次方增长。 有一些技巧可以使用高斯图加速边缘测试。 有关更多详细信息，请参阅分离轴测试的改进。 无论如何，边对边测试可以轻松主导整个模拟。

那么这个边边代码是什么样的呢？ 从基本层面来看，它看起来像这样：

for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges)
{
    for (Edge edgeB : hullB.edges)
    {
        TestCrossProduct(edgeA, edgeB);
    }
}

如果每个船体中只有 12 条边，则对此执行 SIMD 并不是一个很大的胜利，因为 SIMD 需要一些设置工作。 为了使 SIMD 正常工作，数据需要采用数组结构格式 (SoA)。

每艘船体都有 89 条边，故事发生了巨大的变化。 有 7921 次对 TestCrossProduct 的调用。 使用宽 SIMD，我可以同时测试 hullA 的一个边缘与 hullB 的四个边缘。 它看起来有点像这样：

for (Edge edgeA : hullA.edges)
{
    for (EdgeWide edgeWideB : hullB.edgesWide)
    {
        TestCrossProductWide(edgeA, edgeWideB);
    }
}

代码片段中有很多隐藏的细节，如果您好奇，可以查看 Box3D 代码。 但我来这里是为了回答这个问题：我们应该关心 SIMD 来进行碰撞检测吗？ 嗯，我有结果了！ 这些是凸桩基准测试的结果，其中包含 500 步运行的总毫秒数。 我在 AMD 7950x 上测试了 1 到 8 个线程，CPU 频率为 4.42GHz。 结果是 4 次运行中最好的。

线程数 标量 SSE2 AVX2-精简版
1 40706 17337 15762
2 20799 8857 8131
3 13789 5946 5471
4 10324 4509 4084
5 8359 3675 3361
6 6958 3106 2843
7 6006 2697 2477
8 5292 2410 2277

SSE2 的速度是标量的两倍多！ 请记住，这些时序适用于整个模拟，而不仅仅是边沿测试。 标量列还表明接触求解器在标量模式下运行。 这就是模拟的全貌。

Box3D 仅实现 SSE2 内在函数。 在 Box2D 中，我也有 AVX2 内在函数，但有数量惊人的用户没有支持 AVX2 的 CPU。 尽管如此，如 AVX2-Lite 专栏所示，仅在 Box3D 中启用 AVX2 架构即可带来不错的收益。 很高兴看到免费的性能提升。 将来我可能会尝试一次真正的 AVX2 实现测试 8 个边缘。

该基准测试代表具有 89 条边的 32 点船体。 随着分数的进一步增加，SAT 肯定会超支。 幸运的是，Box3D 有 128 条边的硬性限制。 此限制已经存在，并且与保持外壳存储紧凑（8 位索引和每个边缘两个半边缘）有关。 有点令人惊讶的是，将复杂的外壳转换为网格可以解决二次增长问题，但它使形状不太适合动态主体。

您可能想知道 SIMD 边缘测试是否在盒子之间移动了针。 遗憾的是几乎没有效果。 这种优化似乎只对复杂的船体有利。 这种船体可能会在多种情况下出现，例如破坏。 所以它是 Box3D 的一个有价值的补充。

最后，这是视频比较和并排的帧时序。
